title: New App- Q-ARGH! Generate Custom QR Codes and Custom Labels for Monday Items and URLs

posts.author: tio.escarda (Tio Escarda)
posts.cookedHtml:
image1441×581 16.2 KB

Ahoy, Mateys! Announcing a new app for Monday.com: Q-ARGH - Track yer Booty in a sea of Stuff!

Q-ARGH is a light-hearted and light-weight pirate-themed app for Monday.com, built by your scallywag friends at Code Pirates Syndicate. It generates customizable QR Codes for Monday.com Items or URLs, and also allows you to create completely customizable labels for your stuff (integrating QRs and other item column data). Works with Items and sub-items. Track inventory, equipment, or work order status. Generate custom QR codes for any URL.

The Story:

As a manufacturing professional, I struggled with bringing the Monday.com environment into the physical world. My company builds custom cabinets and many other wood components, and we have a large and ever changing inventory as well as the need to track products through our production line. We have many pieces of equipment, vehicles, and tooling, all of which I wanted to inventory and track in Monday.com

We built Q-ARGH to solve this issue. The app allows you to create customized labels that pull in column values to data fields, while populating a QR field with a QR Code for the monday.com item. This allows anyone on the shop floor to interact with the item instantly through the Monday.com app.

What does Q-ARGH do?
Q-ARGH consists of a Board View for customizing the apps settings, and then uses several automations/integrations to generate QR codes and labels, triggered by buttons or item creation. The QR Code generated will be your item’s URL. Works with items and Subitems. This allows anyone with access to your monday account to scan the QR code and instantly interact with that item through the Monday.com interface. We also have an integration that lets you generate QRs based off of a URL column, so you can create custom QRs for any URL imaginable.

Q-ARGH Integrations1511×466 79.7 KB

Q-Argh lets you customize the QR code for your labels, including color, density, and logo insert:

Q_ARGH -QR Custom1252×645 65.8 KB

We provide several stock template sizes for labels based around the Brother QL-810W printer, but the sizes are fairly standardized for many label rolls or label sheet sizes. We can help you make the one you need:

Q_ARGH Label Templates1092×604 71.4 KB

We also created a workflow that allows you to design your own labels using data fields from your columns. Map your column data to a field number, then go crazy designing your own custom label for your needs:

Q-Argh - Data Mapping1312×701 76.2 KB

Q-ARGH- Custom Labels1388×575 56.4 KB

How might you use it?

-Material Execution System (Track status of work orders through your manufacturing process)

-Inventory Tracking (Label locations and items)

-Equipment tracking and maintenance (Add QR code labels for tooling and vehicles, stick them to the object, and allow maintenance team or tool users to update status and location of equipment). Great for manufacturers, construction professionals, rental companies, hotels, etc.

-Flyer Campaigns (Generate QRs for landing deck URL for flyers, letters, emails, etc.)

-Monday.com Form Linkage (Generate QRs for Monday.com forms to utilize in your email or flyer campaigns)

-Signage (QR codes for your signage, restaurant menus, etc. Direct customers to the URL of your choice with a custom QR Code with your logo and color scheme).

-Any use case you can think of that requires a custom QR code or QR Code label! Bring your monday.com environment into the physical world.

Please check us out and sign up for a free trial today. After the trial, we have several plans to fit your use case. We are looking to improve this app to provide the functionality your team needs.

Looking forward to casting off with you on a voyage of organization. Let’s track your stuff!

track_booty1792×1024 302 KB
